Synoptic triangle

Matthew ↔ Mark · Matthew ↔ Luke · overlap

For every verse in Matthew (the gospel in both datasets), the table on this page shows its best parallel in Mark and its best parallel in Luke. Where both are strong, you\'re looking at triple tradition; where only Mark is strong, it\'s Mark-Matt private material; where only Luke is strong, it\'s Q / Luke-Matt only. This is the one page that quietly joins the two datasets.

Coverage map

Matthew\'s 28 chapters laid out as a strip. Each cell is a verse and its best score in Mark (top half, red) and in Luke (bottom half, blue). Amber where both are strong (triple tradition).

Summary counts

Scatter: score(Mark) vs score(Luke)

Each dot is a Matthean verse plotted by its best Mark score and best Luke score. Upper-right = strong triple-tradition. Right-only = Mark-Matt only. Top-only = Luke-Matt (Q). Bottom-left = Matt-exclusive.

Verse-by-verse triangle
